jokes.text: And any perfect day involves crying uncontrollably.
jokes.setup: Leslie talking about planning a perfect last day together
jokes.punchline: Her idea of perfection involves uncontrollable crying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - only she would think crying is an essential part of a perfect day. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Perhaps we could skip ahead and just hit the highlights? Ah! The debate about getting a pod-based coffee machine.
jokes.setup: Ben suggests skipping to highlights of their work history
jokes.punchline: Leslie thinks a coffee machine debate was a highlight worth remembering
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's obsession with mundane bureaucratic details - perfectly in character.

jokes.text: 'Anything is possible if you follow your dreams.' Johnny Karate. Except getting a swing fixed. None of us actually work here anymore.
jokes.setup: Andy quotes his inspirational Johnny Karate persona
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts the inspiration with practical reality
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Andy's ability to accidentally deflate his own inspirational moments.

jokes.text: The vote was four to three / Donna said to get rid of me / But Ron was suspicious 'cause my coffee was delicious
jokes.setup: Gary singing a musical number about coffee machine debate
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and awkward lyrics about office politics
jokes.explanation: Pure Gary - creating elaborate musical numbers about mundane office events.

jokes.text: Come on, Garry. You're the mayor now. Have some dignity.
jokes.setup: Gary is still acting like his old self despite being mayor
jokes.punchline: The idea that Gary could ever have dignity
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary joke - the impossibility of him changing even with a prestigious title.

jokes.text: I cannot wait to fix that swing so we can get your little butt right into it. I know it's weird. Whatever. It motivates me.
jokes.setup: Leslie expressing enthusiasm about fixing the swing
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre fixation on Ben's butt as motivation
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic oversharing and weird romantic fixations.

jokes.text: Five points for Hufflepuff.
jokes.setup: Someone gives a correct answer about bureaucratic forms
jokes.punchline: Leslie treats it like Hogwarts house points
jokes.explanation: Leslie turning mundane work into a game, with a pop culture reference that fits her character.

jokes.text: Thanks to Leslie's scrapbook 'Thanks Form the Memories!'
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Leslie's scrapbook title
jokes.punchline: The pun combining 'forms' with 'for' in the song title
jokes.explanation: Clever bureaucratic pun that's exactly the kind of thing Leslie would create for a government scrapbook.

jokes.text: That crazy story about you getting kicked out of En Vogue. Technically, I kicked them out.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions Donna getting kicked out of En Vogue
jokes.punchline: Donna's correction that she kicked them out instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna moment - of course she wouldn't be kicked out, she'd do the kicking. Great character-driven reversal.

jokes.text: You can watch them build the new Space Haystack around the Space Needle.
jokes.setup: Donna describing a Seattle real estate view
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name 'Space Haystack' for what's being built around the Space Needle
jokes.explanation: Absurd Seattle development name that sounds plausible enough to be funny.

jokes.text: I can hardly see the needle anymore. I know. It's kind of the point.
jokes.setup: Joe commenting he can barely see the Space Needle
jokes.punchline: Donna's matter-of-fact confirmation that obscuring it is intentional
jokes.explanation: Deadpan delivery of absurd urban planning logic.

jokes.text: I think it's all that coffee and legal marijuana. Has people wanting to buy houses quickly and irrationally.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the Seattle real estate market moves so fast
jokes.punchline: Donna's theory about coffee and marijuana making people impulsive buyers
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about Seattle stereotypes with Donna's practical perspective.

jokes.text: The school cut the math club... And math. They just don't teach math anymore.
jokes.setup: Joe mentions the school cutting the math club
jokes.punchline: The escalation that they've cut math entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - goes from cutting a club to cutting the entire subject. Absurd but believable educational commentary.

jokes.text: Call Satan's niece.
jokes.setup: Donna answering her phone
jokes.punchline: Her greeting suggesting the caller is literally related to Satan
jokes.explanation: Peak Donna sass - maximum insult with minimum words. Perfect character voice and economy.

jokes.text: Are you gonna buy me a bunch of leather handbags?
jokes.setup: Donna says she's going to 'treat yo' self' with Joe
jokes.punchline: Joe's immediate assumption about what that means
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the 'Treat Yo Self' episodes with Joe knowing exactly what it usually entails.

jokes.text: I single-handedly started that door-knockers earring trend.
jokes.setup: Donna listing her past adventures
jokes.punchline: Taking credit for starting a specific earring trend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna boast - specific enough to be funny, confident enough to be believable from her.

jokes.text: I am calm. I am grateful. I am Craig.
jokes.setup: Craig trying to appear zen and controlled
jokes.punchline: The mantra ending with his own name as if it's a virtue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig moment - his attempt at meditation still centers entirely on himself. Great structure and character integration.

jokes.text: And then I thought, 'Oh, no. He's insane.' 'That person is psychotic, and I need to call the police.'
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her evolving impressions of Craig
jokes.punchline: The escalation to thinking he was dangerous enough to call police
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Craig's intensity through Leslie's eyes, with her characteristic honesty.

jokes.text: My name is Craig Middlebrooks. I'm the part-time sommelier here at Tom's Bistro. And I'm a very good singer!
jokes.setup: Craig introducing himself in what appears to be a confessional
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific job title and self-aggrandizing declaration about singing
jokes.explanation: Pure Craig - the combination of random specific job and confident self-assessment.

jokes.text: Do you have any regrets? Not one. Not a single one. Do you have any regrets? Are you kidding? Thousands! Most recently, this trout!
jokes.setup: Craig asks his husband if he has regrets, gets 'none'
jokes.punchline: Craig immediately admits to thousands, including the current meal
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between the partners and perfect Craig neurosis - even regretting his current meal.

jokes.text: Shotgun! W-we're just taking the elevator. I get to push the buttons! I called it!
jokes.setup: Andy calls shotgun for going to the fourth floor
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan clarification they're just taking an elevator, Andy doubling down on button privileges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - treating mundane activities like adventures, with April's reality check.

jokes.text: Get with the times, you Luddite.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Ethel to file something the old way
jokes.punchline: Ancient Ethel Beavers calling someone else a Luddite
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the oldest, most traditional character calling others behind the times. Great character reversal.

jokes.text: That sapphire has the power to activate the Cuban Missile Crisis!
jokes.setup: Andy as Agent Macklin describing a dangerous sapphire
jokes.punchline: The gemstone somehow having the power to restart a historical event
jokes.explanation: Peak Andy absurdist logic - treating a historical event like it could be reactivated by a gemstone.

jokes.text: Wait. Seriously? I -- 'cause I will go do that right now.
jokes.setup: April suggests they make love during their roleplay
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately breaking character to confirm this is real
jokes.explanation: Andy's eager, puppyish response to romantic possibility, breaking the spy fantasy immediately.

jokes.text: Did you eat all the candy? No! Did you? Yes. But that's not what's wrong.
jokes.setup: April asks if Andy ate all the Halloween candy
jokes.punchline: Andy's denial followed immediately by admission, then dismissing it as not the real problem
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - can't maintain a lie for even one sentence, plus the suggestion that eating all the candy isn't the main issue.

jokes.text: I want to put a babe in you, babe.
jokes.setup: Andy wanting to talk about having children
jokes.punchline: His awkward phrasing using 'babe' both as endearment and baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy wordplay - accidentally creating a pun while trying to be romantic about having kids.

jokes.text: Or even a little... Boy April.
jokes.setup: Andy wanting a little April
jokes.punchline: His follow-up suggestion of a 'Boy April'
jokes.explanation: Andy's simple logic about names - if April is a girl's name, then Boy April must be the male version.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm Sandra Dee... O'Connor. Get it? Sandra Dee plus Sandra Day O'Connor? Who's Sandra Dee? Who's Sandra Day O'Connor? Forget it.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her clever costume mashup
jokes.punchline: April and Andy not knowing either reference, Leslie giving up
jokes.explanation: Leslie's elaborate costume concept completely lost on the younger generation. Good escalation of frustration.

jokes.text: And I am the Lamplighter, a character from my new board-game sequel 'Cones of Dunshire: Winds of Tremorrah.' Gameplay Magazine called it 'punishingly intricate.'
jokes.setup: Ben explaining his costume
jokes.punchline: The absurdly complex game name and 'punishingly intricate' review
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerdiness - creating a sequel with an even more ridiculous name and treating a negative review as praise.

jokes.text: All right. Let's play. Really? No.
jokes.setup: Ben excited that April wants to play his game
jokes.punchline: April immediately revealing she was lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect April move - brief moment of hope followed by immediate crushing of dreams. Great economy.

jokes.text: Granted, I'd just face-planted on my rollerblades. That's what they were laughing at.
jokes.setup: Andy talking about being moved by children laughing and playing
jokes.punchline: Revealing they were actually laughing at his accident
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - recontextualizes his emotional moment as just kids laughing at his clumsiness.

jokes.text: Take one of our kids. We don't need all three.
jokes.setup: Andy worried about not having kids
jokes.punchline: Ben casually offering to give away one of his children
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan solution to Andy's problem. Great economy and the casual attitude makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You think that's the only time we had sex? Yes!
jokes.setup: April suggesting Ben and Leslie only had sex once to get three kids
jokes.punchline: April confidently confirming this ridiculous assumption
jokes.explanation: April's matter-of-fact response to Leslie's incredulity about their sex life. Great awkward comedy.

jokes.text: But Andy and I are cool already, so our kids will be, like, really lame and weird.
jokes.setup: April explaining that kids are opposite of their parents
jokes.punchline: Her logic that their cool kids would therefore be lame
jokes.explanation: April's twisted logic about inheritance, combined with her self-aware assessment of being cool.

jokes.text: You have kids because you and Andy are a team, and you want to bring in some new team members.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving April advice about having children
jokes.punchline: Framing procreation as team expansion
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic way of turning everything into a team/government metaphor. Sweet and funny.

jokes.text: Burt Macklin Jr.? Mm. We need a Halloween angle. How about Demon Spawn Baby Satan Dwyer?
jokes.setup: Discussing baby names with a Halloween theme
jokes.punchline: April's extremely dark name suggestion
jokes.explanation: Peak April darkness - turning baby naming into something from a horror movie. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Maybe Burt Snakehole Ludgate... Karate Dracula Macklin Demon Jack-o'-Lantern Dwyer? We call him Jack for short?
jokes.setup: Andy trying to incorporate all their personas into a baby name
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously long name simplified to 'Jack'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy logic - combining every fictional character they've ever played into one name, then casually shortening it. Perfect absurdist escalation.

jokes.text: Listen to me very carefully. I don't know who you are or what you want, but I have a particular set of skills...
jokes.setup: Andy trying to convince the doctor to change the birth certificate
jokes.punchline: Using the 'Taken' movie monologue for a birth certificate request
jokes.explanation: Andy using an action movie threat for the most benign possible request. Great character inappropriateness.

jokes.text: Welcome to the team, little guy.
jokes.setup: Leslie meeting the newborn baby
jokes.punchline: Immediately welcoming him to 'the team'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Leslie's earlier advice about adding team members. Shows her consistency.

jokes.text: Will you pretend to be my wife for an insurance scam? But then we fall in love for real! Also, can I have a pair of your gym socks?
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio asking Leslie for a final favor
jokes.punchline: Escalating from insurance fraud to romance to sock fetish
jokes.explanation: Perfectly escalating Jean-Ralphio chaos - from crime to romance to weird personal request. Great character finale.

jokes.text: It's not for anything weird. It's just a fetish I have.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio clarifying his sock request
jokes.punchline: Saying it's not weird, then immediately admitting it's a fetish
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction logic - denying weirdness while confirming it. Great Jean-Ralphio obliviousness.

jokes.text: Don't, don't be suspicious. Don't be suspicious.
jokes.setup: The twins trying to sneak out of the hospital
jokes.punchline: Loudly chanting about not being suspicious while being completely obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - trying to avoid suspicion in the most suspicious way possible. Great twin synchronization.

jokes.text: Yeah, Yoko had a real killer bod. Just like you.
jokes.setup: Jamm giving Leslie a parting gift
jokes.punchline: His creepy comparison of Leslie to Yoko Ono's body
jokes.explanation: Classic Jamm inappropriate comment disguised as a compliment. Perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Can you at least call me Ben? No can do, accounting nerd.
jokes.setup: Ben asking Tom to use his name instead of 'accounting nerd'
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate refusal and doubling down on the nickname
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom-Ben dynamic - Tom refusing to show Ben basic respect even when he's a congressman.

jokes.text: Hey, all my data suggests that the country is definitely trending calzone. Yeah. Ugh. The worst.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting adding calzones to Tom's menu
jokes.punchline: April's immediate disgusted reaction to calzones
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Ben's calzone love with April representing the audience's opinion of them.

jokes.text: Today, Tom Haverford is broke, destitute, and worst of all, swaggerless.
jokes.setup: Documentary narrator describing Tom's business failure
jokes.punchline: The escalation that being 'swaggerless' is worse than poverty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character analysis - for him, losing swagger would indeed be worse than financial ruin. Brilliant escalation.

jokes.text: Who could have predicted that the country would run out of beef?
jokes.setup: Lucy explaining why Tom's restaurant failed
jokes.punchline: The completely unpredictable national beef shortage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist excuse for business failure - a national beef shortage is so random it's funny.

jokes.text: Did you see '10 fast 10 Furious: Tokyo Drift 3?' I did not. No. Well, you might be the only one. It made $2.9 billion.
jokes.setup: Tom referencing a movie title
jokes.punchline: The absurd title and even more absurd box office number
jokes.explanation: Great parody of action movie sequels with the ridiculous title and impossible earnings figure.

jokes.text: 'Sniper Race Delta: Beijing Inferno.'
jokes.setup: Tom revealing his action movie title
jokes.punchline: The completely generic action movie title combining every cliche
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody action movie title - hits every generic action movie word. Pure Tom trying to game the system.

jokes.text: But what do we definitely not what to be? A Garry! A Garry! A Garry!
jokes.setup: Tom at his book tour asking what not to be
jokes.punchline: The audience enthusiastically chanting about not being Gary
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the show's running gag of Gary being the example of what not to be.

jokes.text: Best wishes. And I mean every word of it.
jokes.setup: Tom giving Leslie a generic book inscription
jokes.punchline: His sincere declaration about the meaningless message
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - treating the most generic possible message as deeply meaningful.

jokes.text: As president of City Council, I, Councilwoman Brandi Maxxx, star of more than 11,000 adult films in the last two years,
jokes.setup: Brandi Maxxx conducting official city business
jokes.punchline: Her casual mention of producing impossibly many adult films
jokes.explanation: Classic Brandi moment - the ridiculous production numbers mentioned matter-of-factly during official business.

jokes.text: Man, Gayle looks amazing.
jokes.setup: At Gary's funeral
jokes.punchline: Someone commenting on Gayle's appearance even at her husband's funeral
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the running gag about Gayle being inexplicably attractive, even at the most inappropriate time.

jokes.text: They spelled his name wrong on the tombstone. Ah... Close enough.
jokes.setup: Someone noticing Gary's name is misspelled on his tombstone
jokes.punchline: Ron's casual acceptance of this final indignity
jokes.explanation: Perfect final Gary joke - even in death, his name is still wrong and people just accept it. Great series callback.

jokes.text: I had met a guy online, and we started chatting a lot, and I really liked him, but he turned out to be a computer program that a scientist invented to test the gullibility of human women.
jokes.setup: Shauna explaining her latest romantic disaster
jokes.punchline: The extremely specific and ridiculous catfishing scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shauna romantic disaster - so specific and absurd it's believable for her character.

jokes.text: He texted me, 'game running into overtime. I'll try and stop by.'
jokes.setup: Woman explaining why her fiancé left her at the altar
jokes.punchline: The casual, low-effort breakup text prioritizing a game
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful casual attitude toward abandoning someone at their wedding for sports.

jokes.text: I calculate the cost of my labor at $38.
jokes.setup: Ron finishing the swing repair
jokes.punchline: His extremely specific and low billing for expert craftsmanship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - precise, practical, and undervaluing his own skilled work. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Don't get emotional, Vaughn. You're embarrassing yourself.
jokes.setup: Someone getting emotional about Ron leaving
jokes.punchline: Ron's characteristic dismissal of emotional displays
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron emotional avoidance, treating normal human reactions as weakness.

jokes.text: Congratulations. You now own 51% of the Lagavulin Distillery.
jokes.setup: Ron asking Ben for investment advice
jokes.punchline: Ben's suggestion resulting in owning Ron's favorite whiskey company
jokes.explanation: Perfect investment for Ron - owning his favorite thing. Great character knowledge and callback.

jokes.text: I mean, we were workplace proximity acquaintances for many years.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his relationship with Ben
jokes.punchline: His clinical description of what was clearly friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron emotional avoidance - reducing friendship to bureaucratic terminology. Excellent character voice.

jokes.text: Move. Need the congressman. This is time-sensitive.
jokes.setup: Jen interrupting a social gathering
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically blunt and urgent political business approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jen Barkley moment - treating social events as obstacles to political business.

jokes.text: Yes. Neither can anyone else.
jokes.setup: Gary saying he can't believe he became mayor
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact agreement that it's unbelievable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron honesty - not even pretending Gary's success makes sense. Great economy.

jokes.text: Did you use an egg donor or give Chris a hall pass for a night? Once again, they are my biological children. Once again, I don't believe you.
jokes.setup: April questioning how Ann's kids could be so awesome
jokes.punchline: April's persistent refusal to accept biological parentage
jokes.explanation: Classic April-Ann dynamic - April's backhanded compliment about Ann's kids being too good to be hers.

jokes.text: Ann, you rainbow-infused space unicorn... Ann, you beautiful, sassy mannequin-come-to-life... Ann, you opalescent tree shark.
jokes.setup: Leslie greeting Ann with compliments
jokes.punchline: The increasingly bizarre and nonsensical comparisons
jokes.explanation: Peak Leslie-Ann friendship moment - escalating absurd compliments that somehow feel loving. Great callback structure.

jokes.text: Yeah, we know what that means -- More smart room for it.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying the baby has a large head
jokes.punchline: Andy's interpretation that it means 'smart room'
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy malapropism - 'smart room' instead of brain space. Perfect innocent logic.

jokes.text: I guess what I'm saying is, I'm out of your league, and you need to move on. What? Sorry. I stopped listening when you called me sugarpea.
jokes.setup: Tom giving Donna a self-aggrandizing speech
jokes.punchline: Donna admitting she stopped listening at the pet name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom-Donna dynamic - his ego speech completely ignored because of one weird word choice.

jokes.text: I took the quiz in your book about what kind of person I am. I'm a Ron. Ha. I'm usually a Tom, but sometimes, I'm a Don.
jokes.setup: Ron taking Tom's personality quiz
jokes.punchline: Tom somehow testing as himself and Donna, but Donna also being a Don
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about personality types where Ron is himself but Tom somehow tests as different people.

jokes.text: 3% off. Oh, my God. You're too generous, Tom.
jokes.setup: Tom offering friends and family discount
jokes.punchline: The pathetically small discount and Leslie's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom cheapness and Leslie's sarcastic response. The tiny discount for his closest friends is very in character.

jokes.text: A [Bleep] Library?
jokes.setup: Learning the campus library will bear her name
jokes.punchline: Leslie's shocked/delighted reaction to having a library named after her
jokes.explanation: Leslie's pure joy at having a library named after her - perfect character moment for a book lover.

jokes.text: Hey, babe, for this picture, should I be Burt Macklin, Johnny Karate, or my new character, Sergeant Thunderfist, M.D.?
jokes.setup: Andy asking April which character to be for the photo
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous new character name 'Sergeant Thunderfist, M.D.'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character creation - combining military, martial arts, and medical titles into one absurd persona.
